response:
Here are some angry moms limericks:

---

A mother who'd had quite enough
Said, "This house has been treated too rough!
I've picked up your socks,
Your LEGOS and blocks,
And NOW you want MORE of my stuff?!"

---

There once was a mom on the phone
Who asked to be left all alone
But her kids wouldn't stop
Till she blew her top
And they scattered like birds that had flown

---

A tired mom yelled up the stairs,
"I am NOT picking up all your wares!
I've told you FIVE times,
Now I'm done with your crimes,
Go clean up your room — or else BEWARE!"

---

A mom found a sock in the fridge,
A shoe on the garden wall's ridge,
She counted to ten,
Then counted again,
And drove slowly away off a bridge*

*(*metaphorically!*)

---

"I JUST mopped this floor!" hollered Sue,
As her kids tracked in mud, one by two,
She grabbed the mop tight,
With fury and might,
And muttered things nobody knew

---

Hope these gave you a laugh! 😄
